      aquatic_estimate < 0 & terrestrial_estimate > 0 ~ "Q2",
      aquatic_estimate < 0 & terrestrial_estimate < 0 ~ "Q3",
      aquatic_estimate > 0 & terrestrial_estimate < 0 ~ "Q4"
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(region, quad_raw) %>%
  summarise(n = n_distinct(iso3c), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  mutate(
    prop = n / sum(n),
    quadrant = factor(
      quad_raw,
      levels = c("Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4"),
      labels = c("Aquatic + / Terrestrial +",
                 "Aquatic - / Terrestrial +",
                 "Aquatic - / Terrestrial -",
                 "Aquatic + / Terrestrial -")
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Plot parameters
max_val   <- 0.75
half_width <- 0.2

# Build spike polygons
# r = prop * max_val * sqrt(2) ensures that at prop=1, the spike tip's
# x and y components each equal max_val (the axis limit)
spike_df <- quad_df %>%
  mutate(
    center_angle = case_when(
      quad_raw == "Q1" ~ pi / 4,
      quad_raw == "Q2" ~ 3 * pi / 4,
      quad_raw == "Q3" ~ 5 * pi / 4,
      quad_raw == "Q4" ~ 7 * pi / 4
    ),
    r       = prop * max_val * sqrt(2),
    x_left  = r * cos(center_angle - half_width),
    y_left  = r * sin(center_angle - half_width),
    x_right = r * cos(center_angle + half_width),
    y_right = r * sin(center_angle + half_width)
  ) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  reframe(
    region   = region,
    quadrant = quadrant,
    prop     = prop,
    x        = c(0, x_left, x_right),
    y        = c(0, y_left, y_right),
    group    = paste(region, quadrant)
  )

# Set color scale for plot
quad_colors <- c(
  "Aquatic + / Terrestrial +" = "#2a9d8f",
  "Aquatic - / Terrestrial +" = "#e76f51",
  "Aquatic - / Terrestrial -" = "#6d3b47",
  "Aquatic + / Terrestrial -" = "#457b9d" 
)

# Create plot
(plot <- ggplot(spike_df) +
  geom_polygon(
    aes(x = x, y = y, fill = quadrant, group = group),
    alpha = 0.85
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.4, colour = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.4, colour = "black") +
  facet_wrap(~ region) +
  coord_equal(xlim = c(-max_val, max_val), ylim = c(-max_val, max_val)) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(-max_val, max_val),
    labels = c("75%", "75%"),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = c(-max_val, max_val),
    labels = c("75%", "75%"),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = quad_colors) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)) +
  labs(
    x    = "Percentage of countries",
    y    = "Percentage of countries",
    fill = "Quadrant"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 8) +
  theme(
    panel.grid            = element_blank(),
    panel.background      = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
    plot.background       = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
    strip.background      = element_blank(),
    strip.text            = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold"),
    axis.text             = element_text(size = 6, color = "black"),
    axis.title            = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold"),
    legend.position       = "bottom",
    legend.box            = "vertical",
    legend.title          = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold"),
    legend.text           = element_text(size = 6),
    legend.key.size       = unit(0.35, "cm"),
    legend.spacing        = unit(0.1, "cm"),
    legend.background     = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
    legend.box.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
    panel.spacing         = unit(1, "lines")
  ))

# Save plot
ggsave("../images/SI/quadrant_bar_region_SI.jpg", plot = plot, device = "jpg", units = "in", width = 5, height = 3.5)
```

```{r collinearity plot}
# Significance stars — flag |r| > 0.7 as potentially collinear
upper_melt <- upper_melt %>%
  mutate(
    label     = sprintf("%.2f", value),
    flag_high = abs(value) >= 0.7
  )

# Bold asterisk on values where |r| >= 0.7 to flag high collinearity
  geom_text(
    data   = upper_melt %>% filter(flag_high),
    aes(x = Var2, y = Var1),
    label  = "",
    size   = 3.5,
    color  = "grey20",
    nudge_x = 0.28,
    nudge_y = 0.22
  ) 
```

```{r extra code for posterior map}


# theme_realm_panel <- function() {
#   theme_minimal(base_size = 8) +
#     theme(
#       panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
#       panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
#       panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = scales::alpha("black", 0.25), linewidth = 0.3),
#       panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
#       panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
#       plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
#       axis.text = element_text(size = 6, color = "black"),
#       axis.title = element_blank(),
#       legend.position = "bottom",
#       legend.title = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
#       legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
#       legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),
#       legend.box = "vertical",
#       legend.spacing = unit(0.1, "cm"),
#       legend.margin = margin(t = 0, b = 0),
#       legend.box.margin = margin(t = -2, b = -2),
#       legend.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
#       legend.box.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA)
#     )
# }

# make_realm_map <- function(model_name, df = intecrept_df) {
# 
#   # Filter model + significance flag
#   df_model <- df %>%
#     filter(model == model_name) %>%
#     mutate(significant = (lo95 > 0 & hi95 > 0) | (lo95 < 0 & hi95 < 0))
# 
#   # Join model values to realm geometry
#   geom_joined <- realm_geom_robin %>%
#     left_join(df_model, by = "realm") %>%
#     filter(!is.na(st_is_empty(geometry)) & !st_is_empty(geometry))
# 
#   ggplot() +
#     # Basemap (always visible)
#     geom_sf(
#       data = continent_basemap,
#       fill = "darkgray",
#       color = "grey70",
#       size = 0.2
#     ) +
# 
#     # Realm shading (outline only for non-NA)
#     geom_sf(
#       data = geom_joined,
#       aes(fill = med, color = !is.na(med)),
#       size = 0.2
#     ) +
#     scale_color_manual(
#       values = c("TRUE" = "grey30", "FALSE" = NA),
#       guide = "none"
#     ) +
# 
#     # Significance outlines
#     geom_sf(
#       data = geom_joined %>% filter(significant),
#       fill = NA,
#       color = "#1F1F1F",
#       linewidth = 0.5
#     ) +
# 
#     # Global symmetric fill scale (computed outside this function)
#     scale_fill_gradient2(
#       low = "#b2182b",
#       mid = "grey85",
#       high = "#2166ac",
#       midpoint = 0,
#       limits = c(-global_limit, global_limit),
#       oob = scales::squish
#     ) +
# 
#     # Hide legend inside each subplot
#     theme_realm_panel() +
#     theme(legend.position = "none") +
# 
#     labs(
#       title = paste(model_name),
#       fill = "Posterior\nmedian\nintercept"
#     )
# }
# 
# 
# # Produce maps for all models
# all_models <- unique(intecrept_df$model)
# realm_maps <- map(all_models, ~ make_realm_map(.x))
# names(realm_maps) <- all_models
# 
# # Create individual panels
# p1 <- realm_maps[["animal_cons_cap"]] +
#   labs(title = "Daily animal protein consumption per capita") +
#   theme_realm_panel() +
#   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 8, face = "bold"))
# 
# p2 <- realm_maps[["aquatic_rel"]] +
#   labs(title = "Aquatic animal reliance per capita") +
#   theme_realm_panel() +
#   theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 8, face = "bold"))
# 
# # Create combined panel plot
# (plot <- (p1 | p2) +
#   plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
#   theme(
#     legend.position = "bottom",
#     aspect.ratio = 0.45,   # ← try 0.40–0.55 depending on taste
#     plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0) 
#   ))

# Save plot
# ggsave(paste0("../images/", model_type, "/intercept_map.jpg"), plot = plot, device = "jpg", width = 5, height = 4, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```

```{r create ppd plot}
# Read in posterior predictive distrbituion data
ppd_all_models <- read_parquet(
  paste0("../output/bayesian_output/", model_type, "/posterior_predictive_all_models.parquet")
)

# Get letters for panel numbers
panel_tags <- letters[1:15] 

# Function to draw a single panel ---
make_ppd_panel <- function(df, title, tag = "") {
  ggplot(df, aes(x = ppd, group = draw)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.08, color = "grey60") +
    geom_density(aes(x = y_obs), color = "black", linewidth = 1.1) +
    labs(y = "Density") +
    annotate(
      "text",
      x = -Inf, y = Inf,
      label = tag,
      hjust = -0.3, vjust = 1.3,
      size = 3, fontface = "bold"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 8) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = scales::alpha("black", 0.25), linewidth = 0.3),
      panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
      panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
      strip.background = element_blank(),
      strip.text = element_blank(),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 6, color = "black"),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 7, margin = margin(r = 5), face = "bold"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 5.5, angle = 30, hjust = 1),
      axis.title.x = element_blank()
    )
}

#  Split df_plot into 9 panels
xlab_plot <- ggplot() +
  theme_void() +
  annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5,
           label = "Response value",
           size = 2.5, fontface = "bold")

draw_sample <- sample(unique(ppd_all_models$draw), size = 30)

df_plot <- ppd_all_models %>%
  filter(
    model != "animal_cons_cap",
    str_detect(clean_name, "capita") |
      str_detect(clean_name, "Aquatic reliance") |
      str_detect(clean_name, "reliance")
  ) %>%
  filter(draw %in% draw_sample) %>%
  mutate(clean_name = factor(clean_name))

panel_names <- levels(df_plot$clean_name)

layout_order <- c(15, 3, 5,
                  1, 7, 9,
                  13, 11, 4,
                  2, 6, 8,
                  10, 14, 12)

panel_tags <- letters[1:15]

# assign tags in layout order
tags_by_panel_index <- rep("", length(panel_names))
tags_by_panel_index[layout_order] <- panel_tags

# Create panel plots
panels <- lapply(seq_along(panel_names), function(i) {
  nm <- panel_names[i]
  df_sub <- df_plot %>% filter(clean_name == nm)
  make_ppd_panel(df_sub, nm, tag = tags_by_panel_index[i])
})


# Create empty plots for bottom row in the combined plot (so final plot that sits alone at the bottom can be centered)
empty <- ggplot() + theme_void()

# Create panels that will display in the ppd plot
row1 <- panels[[15]]  | panels[[3]]  | panels[[5]]
row2 <- panels[[1]]  | panels[[7]]  | panels[[9]]
row3 <- panels[[13]]  | panels[[11]]  | panels[[4]]
row4 <- panels[[2]] | panels[[6]] | panels[[8]]
row5 <- panels[[10]] | panels[[14]] | panels[[12]]
row6 <- empty | xlab_plot | empty

(final_plot <- wrap_plots(
  row1, row2, row3, row4, row5, row6,
  ncol = 1
) + plot_layout(heights = c(1,1,1,1,1,0.15)))

ggsave(paste0("../images/SI/ppd_SI.jpg"), plot = final_plot, device = "jpg", width = 4, height = 5, units = "in", dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
```{r ppd distributions}
# Variable list with coastline_km area instead of EEZ area
predictors <- c(
  "coastline_km",
  "log_gdp",
  "gdp_trade",
  "inundation_area_log1000km2",
  "supermarkets",
  "pop_10k_pct_coast"
)

# Variable list with EEZ total area instead of coastline_km
# predictors <- c(
#   "eez_total",
#   "log_gdp",
#   "gdp_trade",
#   "inundation_area_log1000km2",
#   "supermarkets",
#   "pop_10k_pct_coast"
# )

# Get which random effect is used
model_type = "realm"

# Get which variable is selected (for unique file path)
var_used = predictors[1]

# Create file path to save files to
base_path <- file.path(
  "..", "output", "bayesian_output",
  paste0("random_effect_", model_type),
  paste0("var_", var_used)
)

if ( !file.exists(paste0("../output/bayesian_output/", model_type, "/posterior_predictive_all_models.parquet")) ) {

# Load in function to clean the model output names from snake case to a publication-friendly read
source("../R/clean_model_name.R")

# Function to extract posterior predictive draws for a model.
extract_ppd <- function(model_name, model_type) {

  # Load fitted Stan model
  fit <- readRDS(file.path(base_path, paste0("fit_", response_vars[i], ".rds")))

  x <- rstan::extract(fit, permuted = TRUE)

  # Posterior predictive draws (S × N)
  y_pred <- x$y_pred

  # Load X and y to get N
  X <- readRDS(file.path(base_path, paste0("X_", response_vars[i], ".rds")))
  y <- readRDS(file.path(base_path, paste0("y_", response_vars[i], ".rds")))

  N <- length(y)

  # Load realm names (vector of length N)
  realm_levels <- readRDS(file.path(base_path, paste0("realms_", response_vars[i], ".rds")))

  # Safety check
  if (length(realm_levels) != N) {
    stop(paste0("realm_levels length mismatch for model ", model_name))
  }

  # Format long
  ppd_long <- as.data.frame(y_pred) %>%
    mutate(draw = row_number()) %>%
    pivot_longer(-draw, names_to = "obs", values_to = "ppd") %>%
    mutate(
      obs = as.integer(gsub("V", "", obs)),
      region = realm_levels[obs],
      y_obs = y[obs],
      model = model_name
    )

  return(ppd_long)
}


# Loop over all models and bind results
ppd_all_models <- map_dfr(response_vars, ~ extract_ppd(.x, model_type)) %>%
  mutate(clean_name = clean_model_name(model)) # Add column for clean model names


# Calculate posterior predictive means
ppd_means <- ppd_all_models %>%
  group_by(model, obs, region) %>%
  summarise(
    ppd_mean = mean(ppd),
    y_obs    = first(y_obs),
    .groups  = "drop"
  )

}
```

```{r significance outlines for bayesian posterior plots}


    # Non-significant rows — reduced opacity, drawn behind
    # geom_segment(data = df_nsig,
    #              aes(x = lo95, xend = hi95, color = col95),
    #              linewidth = 2.2, lineend = "butt", alpha = 0.3) +
    # geom_segment(data = df_nsig,
    #              aes(x = lo80, xend = hi80, color = col80),
    #              linewidth = 2.2, lineend = "butt", alpha = 0.3) +
    # geom_segment(data = df_nsig,
    #              aes(x = lo50, xend = hi50, color = col50),
    #              linewidth = 2.2, lineend = "butt", alpha = 0.3) +
    # geom_point(data = df_nsig,
    #            aes(x = med), shape = 21, size = 1.6,
    #            fill = "white", color = "grey60", stroke = 0.3, alpha = 0.5) +

    # Significant rows — full opacity, drawn on top
    # geom_segment(data = df_sig,
    #              aes(x = lo95, xend = hi95, color = col95),
    #              linewidth = 2.2, lineend = "butt", alpha = 1) +
    # geom_segment(data = df_sig,
    #              aes(x = lo80, xend = hi80, color = col80),
    #              linewidth = 2.2, lineend = "butt", alpha = 1) +
    # geom_segment(data = df_sig,
    #              aes(x = lo50, xend = hi50, color = col50),
    #              linewidth = 2.2, lineend = "butt", alpha = 1) +
    # geom_point(data = df_sig,
    #            aes(x = med), shape = 21, size = 1.6,
    #            fill = "white", color = "grey20", stroke = 0.4) +
```

```{r residuals}
# Load objects
fit <- readRDS(file.path(base_path, paste0("fit_", response_vars[i], ".rds")))
x   <- rstan::extract(fit, permuted = TRUE)

X <- readRDS(file.path(base_path, paste0("X_", response_vars[i], ".rds")))
y <- readRDS(file.path(base_path, paste0("y_", response_vars[i], ".rds")))
realm <- readRDS(file.path(base_path, paste0("realms_", response_vars[i], ".rds")))

province <- as.integer(factor(realm))

# Dimensions
n_iter <- length(x$beta_0)
N <- length(y)

# Storage
residuals <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_iter, ncol = N)

# Compute residuals
for (s in 1:n_iter) {
  mu_s <- x$beta_0i[s, province] + X %*% x$beta[s, ]
  residuals[s, ] <- y - mu_s
}


resid_mean <- colMeans(residuals)

hist(resid_mean)
qqnorm(resid_mean); qqline(resid_mean)
```

```{r extra model names}
if ( !file.exists("../output/response_vars.rds") ) {
  
  # Calculate animal consumption
terr_animal_cons_cap <- aquatic_reliance %>%
  filter(year == 2019) %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  summarize(protein_consumed_kg_percap = sum(protein_consumed_kg_percap))

animal_cons_cap <- aquatic_reliance %>%
  filter(year == 2019) %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  summarize(protein_consumed_kg_percap = sum(protein_consumed_kg_percap))

# Calculate aquatic animal consumption
aquatic_animal_cons_cap <- aquatic_reliance %>%
  filter(year == 2019, food_group == "aquatic") %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  summarize(protein_consumed_kg_percap = sum(protein_consumed_kg_percap))

# Calculate animal reliance consumption
aquatic_rel <- aquatic_animal_cons_cap %>%
  left_join(animal_cons_cap, by = "iso3c") %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  summarize(prop_animal_protein = protein_consumed_kg_percap.x / protein_consumed_kg_percap.y)

# Add items not created in the loop to the list
response_vars <- c("terr_animal_cons_cap","animal_cons_cap", "aquatic_animal_cons_cap", "aquatic_rel", "aquatic_protein_roc", "terrestrial_protein_roc")

for (i in c("capture", "aquaculture", "domestic", "foreign", "marine", "inland")) {
  
  # Calculate consumption per capita by method
  if (i %in% c("capture", "aquaculture")) {
    assign(
      paste0(i, "_cap"),
      aquatic_reliance %>%
        filter(year == 2019, food_group == "aquatic", method == i) %>%
        group_by(iso3c) %>%
        summarize(protein_consumed_kg_percap = sum(protein_consumed_kg_percap))
    )
  }
  
  # Calculate consumption per capita by consumption source
  if (i %in% c("domestic", "foreign")) {
    assign(
      paste0(i, "_cap"),
      aquatic_reliance %>%
        filter(year == 2019, food_group == "aquatic", consumption_source == i) %>%
        group_by(iso3c) %>%
        summarize(protein_consumed_kg_percap = sum(protein_consumed_kg_percap))
    )
  }
  
  # Calculate consumption per capita by habitat
  if (i %in% c("marine", "inland")) {
    assign(
      paste0(i, "_cap"),
      aquatic_reliance %>%
        filter(year == 2019, food_group == "aquatic", habitat == i) %>%
        group_by(iso3c) %>%
        summarize(protein_consumed_kg_percap = sum(protein_consumed_kg_percap))
    )
  }
  
  response_vars <- c(response_vars, paste0(i, "_cap"))
  
  # Calculate reliance by method, production source, or habitat
  assign(
    paste0(i, "_rel"),
    get(paste0(i, "_cap")) %>%
      left_join(animal_cons_cap, by = "iso3c") %>%
      group_by(iso3c) %>%
      summarize(prop_animal_protein = protein_consumed_kg_percap.x / protein_consumed_kg_percap.y)
  )
  
  response_vars <- c(response_vars, paste0(i, "_rel"))

}

#calculate rate of change in aquatic protein quantity
aquatic_protein_roc <- aquatic_reliance %>%
  filter(data_source == "new FBS", food_group == "aquatic") %>%
  group_by(iso3c, year, method) %>%
  summarize(protein_consumed_kg_percap = sum(protein_consumed_kg_percap, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  filter(method == "capture") %>%
  split(.$iso3c) %>%
  map(~lm(protein_consumed_kg_percap~year, data = .x)) %>%
  map_df(broom::tidy, .id = 'iso3c') %>%
  filter(term == 'year') %>%
  select(c("iso3c", "aquatic_estimate" = "estimate"))

#calculate rate of change in terrestrial protein quantity
terrestrial_protein_roc <- aquatic_reliance %>%
  filter(data_source == "new FBS", food_group == "terrestrial") %>%
  group_by(iso3c, year) %>%
  summarize(protein_consumed_kg_percap = sum(protein_consumed_kg_percap, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  split(.$iso3c) %>%
  map(~lm(protein_consumed_kg_percap~year, data = .x)) %>%
  map_df(broom::tidy, .id = 'iso3c') %>%
  filter(term == 'year') %>%
  select(c("iso3c", "terrestrial_estimate" = "estimate"))

# Save response_vars to RDS format
saveRDS(response_vars, "../data/response_vars.rds")
}
```

### SI Figure: R^2

```{r R^2}
# Calculate hierarchical R² across models, decomposed into fixed, random,
# and combined (conditional) components
R2_df <- data.frame(
  fixed_r2    = numeric(0),
  random_r2   = numeric(0),
  combined_r2 = numeric(0),
  model       = character(0)
)

for (i in seq_along(response_vars)) {

  fit <- rstan::extract(
    readRDS(file.path(base_path, paste0("fit_", response_vars[i], ".rds"))),
    permuted = TRUE
  )

  b_s       <- fit$beta_0i[1:3000, ]
  realm_ids <- readRDS(file.path(base_path, paste0("realms_", response_vars[i], ".rds")))
  X         <- readRDS(file.path(base_path, paste0("X_",      response_vars[i], ".rds")))
  Z         <- model.matrix(~ 0 + factor(realm_ids))

  # Linear predictors: iterations × N
  mu_random <- b_s %*% t(Z)
  beta_s    <- fit$beta[1:3000, ]
  mu_fixed  <- beta_s %*% t(X)

  # Variance partitioning across posterior iterations
  var_fixed_s  <- apply(mu_fixed,  1, var)
  var_random_s <- apply(mu_random, 1, var)
  var_resid_s  <- fit$sigma^2
  mu_total     <- mu_fixed + mu_random
  var_total_s  <- apply(mu_total, 1, var)

  # Marginal R² (fixed), random R², and conditional R² (combined)
  R2_fixed_s  <- var_fixed_s  / (var_total_s + var_resid_s)
  R2_random_s <- var_random_s / (var_total_s + var_resid_s)
  R2_cond_s   <- var_total_s  / (var_total_s + var_resid_s)

  R2_df <- bind_rows(
    R2_df,
    data.frame(
      fixed_r2    = R2_fixed_s,
      random_r2   = R2_random_s,
      combined_r2 = R2_cond_s,
      model       = response_vars[i]
    )
  )
}

# Colours for effect type - drawn from project palette
# Fixed = blue (predictors), random = teal (realm structure), combined = grey
r2_colors <- c(
  "Fixed"    = "#2B6AA8",
  "Random"   = "#1A7A6E",
  "Combined" = "#888780"
)

# Build plot data - filter to capita and reliance models, clean names
r2_plot_df <- R2_df %>%
  filter(
    str_detect(model, "_cap") | str_detect(model, "_rel"),
    model != "animal_cons_cap"
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(fixed_r2, random_r2, combined_r2),
    names_to  = "effect",
    values_to = "r2"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    clean_name = clean_model_name(model),
    effect     = factor(
      case_when(
        effect == "fixed_r2"    ~ "Fixed",
        effect == "random_r2"   ~ "Random",
        effect == "combined_r2" ~ "Combined"
      ),
      levels = c("Fixed", "Random", "Combined")
    )
  )

# Compute posterior medians and 95% CI per model × effect for reference lines
r2_summary <- r2_plot_df %>%
  group_by(clean_name, effect) %>%
  summarise(
    med   = median(r2),
    lo95  = quantile(r2, 0.025),
    hi95  = quantile(r2, 0.975),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Shared theme applied to every individual panel
theme_r2 <- function(show_y = FALSE, show_x = FALSE) {
  base <- theme_minimal(base_size = 8) +
    theme(
      text                  = element_text(family = "sans", color = "black"),
      plot.background       = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
      panel.background      = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = NA),
      panel.grid.minor      = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x    = element_line(
        color = scales::alpha("black", 0.12), linewidth = 0.3
      ),
      panel.grid.major.y    = element_blank(),
      strip.background      = element_blank(),
      plot.title            = element_text(size = 5.5, face = "bold",
                                            hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 2)),
      plot.tag              = element_text(size = 7, face = "bold"),
      plot.tag.position     = "topleft",
      axis.text.x           = element_text(size = 5.5, color = "black"),
      axis.text.y           = element_text(size = 5.5, color = "black"),
      axis.title.x          = element_blank(),
      axis.title.y          = element_blank(),
      legend.position       = "none",
      plot.margin           = margin(3, 4, 3, 4)
    )

  if (show_y) base <- base + theme(
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 6.5, face = "bold", margin = margin(r = 3))
  )
  if (show_x) base <- base + theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 6.5, face = "bold", margin = margin(t = 3))
  )
  base
}

# Build one density panel per model
model_names <- unique(r2_plot_df$clean_name)

panels <- lapply(seq_along(model_names), function(i) {
  nm      <- model_names[i]
  df_sub  <- r2_plot_df %>% filter(clean_name == nm)
  sum_sub <- r2_summary  %>% filter(clean_name == nm)

  # Left column panels: 1, 5, 9, 13 - show y-axis title
  # Bottom row panels: 13, 14, 15 - show x-axis title
  show_y <- i %in% c(1, 5, 9, 13)
  show_x <- i %in% c(13, 14, 15)

  ggplot(df_sub, aes(x = r2, colour = effect, fill = effect)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.18, linewidth = 0.55) +
    geom_vline(
      data      = sum_sub,
      aes(xintercept = med, colour = effect),
      linewidth = 0.4,
      linetype  = "dashed"
    ) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = r2_colors) +
    scale_fill_manual(  values = r2_colors) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      limits = c(0, 1),
      breaks = c(0, 0.5, 1),
      labels = c("0", "0.5", "1")
    ) +
    labs(
      title = nm,
      tag   = LETTERS[i],
      y     = if (show_y) "Density" else NULL,
      x     = if (show_x) bquote("Posterior" ~ R^2) else NULL
    ) +
    theme_r2(show_y = show_y, show_x = show_x)
})

# Extract a shared legend from a single fully-specified panel
legend_source <- ggplot(r2_plot_df, aes(x = r2, colour = effect, fill = effect)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.18, linewidth = 0.55) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = r2_colors, name = "Effect type") +
  scale_fill_manual(  values = r2_colors, name = "Effect type") +
  guides(
    colour = guide_legend(title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0.5, nrow = 1),
    fill   = guide_legend(title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0.5, nrow = 1)
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

legend_grob <- cowplot::get_plot_component(
  legend_source, "guide-box", return_all = TRUE
)
legend_grob <- Filter(function(x) !inherits(x, "zeroGrob"), legend_grob)[[1]]

# Assemble rows manually - bottom row (3 panels) centered with empty spacers
# widths c(0.5, 1, 1, 1, 0.5) flanks the three panels with half-column margins
empty <- ggplot() + theme_void()

row1 <- panels[[1]]  | panels[[2]]  | panels[[3]]  | panels[[4]]
row2 <- panels[[5]]  | panels[[6]]  | panels[[7]]  | panels[[8]]
row3 <- panels[[9]]  | panels[[10]] | panels[[11]] | panels[[12]]
row4 <- (empty | panels[[13]] | panels[[14]] | panels[[15]] | empty) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(0.5, 1, 1, 1, 0.5))

grid_plot <- wrap_plots(row1, row2, row3, row4, ncol = 1) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1, 1, 1)) &
  theme(plot.margin = margin(2, 3, 2, 3))

# Stack grid above shared legend
plot <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  grid_plot,
  legend_grob,
  ncol        = 1,
  rel_heights = c(1, 0.11)
)

ggsave(
  filename = paste0("../images/SI/r2.pdf"),
  plot     = plot,
  device   = cairo_pdf,
  width    = 9,
  height   = 5,
  units    = "in"
)
```
